summary: Tested NEW Marketing Requests Module (Sales→Marketing→Delivery) end-to-end. 19/19 backend pytest cases pass against the production preview URL covering masters auto-seed, lead-time guardrail, MR-YYYY-NNNN numbering, all queue counts, ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS, comments, versions, production-submit (incl. final_approved guard), production-status, file upload/download, and default-protected master deletes. Frontend pages /marketing-requests, /marketing-requests/new and /marketing-requests/:id all render correctly with Sales/Marketing/Delivery tab groups + counts badges, transition buttons, work versions, and comments timeline. Sidebar entry 'Marketing Requests' visible under Sales (verified) and Marketing (verified) module contexts.

minor.issue: Admin override of ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS is intentional (CEO/director can shortcut), but it makes it impossible for an admin-tester to verify the deny-path through the API; non-admin role is needed to assert HTTP 400 on illegal jumps. Server-side gate is otherwise correct.

issues: 'Requested Due Date' uses native HTML <input type="date"> instead of the shadcn Calendar/Popover component used elsewhere in the app — inconsistent UX (system prompt explicitly flags this pattern).

action_items: Optional: Replace native <input type="date"> on NewMarketingRequest.js with the shadcn Calendar/Popover Date Picker for visual consistency.
Optional: Add a role-based negative test for illegal status transitions using a non-admin Marketing user account.
critical_code_review_comments: ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS dict + status_key map is clean and centralised — good.
ObjectStorage uploads stored in collection 'marketing_request_files' with tenant scoping — files endpoint properly tenant-isolated. Good.
Mongo _id is excluded via projection {'_id':0} throughout — good.
Department matching uses title-case + lower-case OR list to tolerate mixed-case user.department values — pragmatic but a normalised collation index would be cleaner long-term.

production-submit transitions to production_in_progress AND sets production.production_status='pending'. The 'production_pending' queue filter therefore picks it up correctly, but the lifecycle status_key is already 'production_in_progress' at that moment — a small naming dissonance (consider keeping status_key='final_approved' until Delivery actually starts work, OR renaming the queue to 'production_assigned').
QUEUE_FILTERS.production_pending requires 'production.production_status: pending' but also relies on lifecycle status_key default; not strictly broken just slightly redundant.
marketing_requests.py is ~553 lines — still within the 700-line guideline but starting to get heavy; future refactor into router + service layer would help.
seed_data_creation: Test created several TEST_QA_* marketing requests + one TEST_QA_DEPT master department (deleted as part of test). MR-2026-000x numbers were consumed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression test suite is /app/backend/tests/test_marketing_requests_lifecycle.py (19 cases). It auto-creates new MR requests so the request_number counter will keep advancing per run. If you need to test role-gated 403 paths, log in as a non-admin user (e.g., a Sales-only role) — admin overrides ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS and most permission gates.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. All required features in the review request are working end-to-end.
